final_answer:
Simple version:

Gilt Chain yield is paid from the chain’s own reward system for people who stake and secure the network.  
It can also add extra rewards later through controlled token issuance, but that switch starts off as off.  
Right now the default model is: GILT stakers get the yield stream.  
GOLD itself is mainly the gold asset layer; just holding GOLD does not automatically earn yield unless you explicitly turn on GOLD reward sharing.

So the core idea is: yield comes from network participation, not from passive gold custody.
